channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: EPA head Regan, who championed environmental justice, to leave office Dec. 31
item.description: Michael Regan, who has led the Environmental Protection Agency throughout President Joe Biden’s four-year term, said Friday he will be leaving the agency Dec. 31, about three weeks before Biden’ leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden canceling student loan debt for 55K public service workers
item.description: President Biden announced on Friday he would be canceling more student debt for tens of thousands of public service workers, possibly his last major move on the issue before leaving office in a month.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Time is running short to avert a government shutdown after funding bill is rejected
item.description: Hours before the start of a federal government shutdown, President-elect Donald Trump doubled-down Friday on his insistence that a debt ceiling increase be included in any deal — and if not, let the closures “start now.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US diplomats and hostage envoy in Syria on first visit since Assad ouster
item.description: The first U.S. diplomats to visit Syria since President Bashar Assad's ouster earlier this month have held talks with transitional officials in Damascus to press for an inclusive government and seek information on the whereabouts of missing American journalist Austin Tice.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In an early legislative test for Trump, plan B spending bill tanks in House
item.description: House Republicans failed to secure the votes needed Thursday on a spending bill amid a looming government shutdown, handing a loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luna wants disaster aid separate from government funding bill
item.description: Hurricane survivors will need to wait a while longer to see additional disaster relief aid come from Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing Washington to the brink of a federal government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says Biden will visit Pope Francis, Meloni in final international trip of his presidency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will travel to Italy next month to meet with Pope Francis and top Italian officials for what is expected to be the final international trip of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New House GOP budget proposal revives Farm Bill extension and aid money but drops ethanol provision
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — American farmers are hoping that aid to agriculture will be revived as Congress struggles to pass a short-term spending bill that would keep the federal government funded and avert a looming partial government shutdown set to begin after midnight Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-backed spending bill goes down in flames as shutdown looms
item.description: The House of Representatives is in chaos amid a last-minute bid to avert a Friday government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon says it doubled the number of US troops in Syria before Assad's fall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States has more than doubled the number of its forces in Syria to fight the Islamic State group — a dramatic increase that the Pentagon revealed Thursday, acknowledging that the added troops have been there for months or even more than a year. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutor Fani Willis is removed from the Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others, the latest legal victory for the president-elect in criminal cases that once threatened his career and freedom.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: With a resounding no, the House rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s new plan Thursday to fund federal operations and suspend the debt ceiling a day before a government shutdown, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: President-elect Trump on Thursday backed a new spending deal brokered by House Republicans to avert a shutdown after an initial proposal was torpedoed amid opposition from Trump and several of his allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken announces aid for Sudan and talks about AI risks at UN Security Council
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken announced $200 million in humanitarian aid for conflict-torn Sudan during what was likely his final trip to the United Nations while in office. With the U.N. Security Council more divided than ever, Blinken led two meetings of the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans agree on Plan B government funding bill with Trump's debt limit increase
item.description: House Republicans have reached an agreement on funding the government before Friday's shutdown deadline. The deal would also suspend the debt limit for two years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans announce funding agreement, Thursday vote as shutdown looms
item.description: House Republicans have reached an agreement on a plan B to avert a government shutdown ahead of Friday’s deadline and plan to vote Thursday, lawmakers announced.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson spending deal throws Speakership into question as floor vote approaches
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson’s handling of an end-of-year spending deal is throwing his grasp on the gavel into uncertain territory ahead of a critical Jan. 3 Speaker vote, as some GOP lawmakers question their support for the Louisiana Republican. Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams surrenders to DA's office to face charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams surrendered to authorities Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to the administration of a mayor who has seen who is facing his own indictment and investigations into multiple aides and allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rand Paul blocks bill to authorize local, state authorities to track drones
item.description: Paul argued the bill would have expanded the government’s surveillance powers and downplayed concerns that the drones pose a threat to the public.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Paxton files motion to block auction of border wall materials
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a motion Tuesday to stop the sale of pieces of the border wall.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico tightens bonds with immigrant community in US
item.description: The government of Mexico is strengthening bonds with its citizens in the United States, pledging to assist them at a time immigrant communities could be coming under increased scrutiny here.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the U.S. if it's not sold
item.description: The justices said Wednesday they will hear arguments Jan. 10 about whether the law impermissibly restricts speech in violation of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Here's what made the cut in Congress’ 1,500-page government funding deal
item.description: Congress on Tuesday rolled out its last major funding deal of the year and is expected to move quickly to try to pass it ahead of a Friday government shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Here's why Trump’s hush money judge rebuffed the immunity claim
item.description: President-elect Trump suffered a major loss in his bid to toss his New York criminal conviction when a judge ruled Monday that the guilty verdict withstands the Supreme Court’s presidential immunity ruling.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: Trump pledged that “The Republican Party will use its best efforts to eliminate Daylight Saving Time"
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: DHS rolls out new rules for skilled foreign workers on H-1B visas
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security on Tuesday announced new rules for hiring foreign workers that was met with praise for allowing employers to fill jobs quicker and maintaining strict security oversight
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Angie Craig chosen as top Democrat on U.S. House Agriculture Committee
item.description: Minnesota 2nd District Rep. Angie Craig was elected Tuesday to serve as the top Democrat on the U.S. House Agriculture Committee.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump weighs in on NYC mayor, vaccines and drones in freewheeling press conference at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump told reporters that he would consider pardoning embattled New York Mayor Eric Adams, said the country was “not going to lose” the polio vaccine and weighed in on the flurry of drone sightings over New Jersey during a freewheeling press conference Monday at his Mar-a-Lago
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5
